sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 14.38 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.14)

statement.latex: For every pro-$p$-group$G$of$(2 2)$-matrices for$p 2$an analogue of the Tits Alternative holds: either$G$is soluble, or the variety of pro-$p$-groups generated by$G$contains the group {${ < ( \!\! {cc}1&t 0&1 \!\! ),\, (\!\! {cc}1&0 t&1 \!\! ) > }$}\,$SL_2({ F} _p[[t]])$(A.\,N.\,Zubkov, Algebra and Logic, 29, no.\,4 (1990), 287--301). Is the same result true for matrices of size$3$for$p 2$?

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
